findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Path containment check in IO/LoadPaths without canonicalization
 * @description Finds startsWith path checks in IO and LoadPaths files without canonicalization
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/load-paths-startswith
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "String") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("LoadPaths")
select mc, "String.startsWith() path check in LoadPaths - verify canonicalization occurs before this check"
findings.hypothesis: LoadPaths uses String.startsWith() for path containment without proper canonicalization

message.text: String.startsWith() path check in LoadPaths - verify canonicalization occurs before this check

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoadPaths path canonicalization analysis
 * @description Check if getCanonicalPath or normalize is called before startsWith in LoadPaths
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/load-paths-canon-analysis
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

// Find all method calls in LoadPaths class
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "LoadPaths"
  and (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getAbsolutePath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("normalize") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("cleanPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("canonical") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toAbsolutePath")
  )
select mc, mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " - line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - call: " + mc.getMethod().getName()
findings.hypothesis: Analyzing whether canonicalization happens before startsWith in LoadPaths

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Detailed analysis of isFileWithinDirectory method in LoadPaths
 * @description Examine what operations happen in isFileWithinDirectory and surrounding methods
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/load-paths-isfilewithin
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() = "isFileWithinDirectory" and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "LoadPaths"
select mc, "In isFileWithinDirectory: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Understanding what isFileWithinDirectory does - is path canonicalized before startsWith check?

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name ClassPathResource analysis for protocol/scheme injection
 * @description Examine ClassPathResource for URL scheme injection vulnerabilities
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/classpath-resource-protocol
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-073
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "ClassPathResource"
  and (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getResource") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getResourceAsStream") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("contains") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("cleanPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("normalize") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toURL") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toURI")
  )
select mc, "ClassPathResource." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: ClassPathResource may have protocol injection or path traversal vulnerabilities

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Zip slip - ZipEntry name used to construct file paths without validation
 * @description ZipEntry.getName() used in File constructor without checking for path traversal
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/zip-slip
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

from MethodCall getName, ClassInstanceExpr fileNew
where
  getName.getMethod().hasName("getName") and
  getName.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.util.zip", "ZipEntry") and
  fileNew.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
  DataFlow::localFlow(DataFlow::exprNode(getName), DataFlow::exprNode(fileNew.getAnArgument()))
select fileNew, "ZipEntry.getName() flows to File constructor - potential zip slip vulnerability"
findings.hypothesis: Zip slip - ZipEntry getName flowing to File constructor without traversal check

message.text: ZipEntry.getName() flows to File constructor - potential zip slip vulnerability
ZipEntry.getName() flows to File constructor - potential zip slip vulnerability

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Zipper zip slip - check if containment validation exists around ZipEntry.getName() to File
 * @description Check what validation exists near the zip slip locations
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/zipper-validation-check
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

// Find all method calls in the methods containing zip slip
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "Zipper" and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("contains") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("normalize") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getAbsolutePath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getAbsoluteFile")
  ) and
  (
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName().matches("%nzip%") or
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName().matches("%unzip%") or
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName().matches("%extract%") or
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName().matches("%Unzip%") or
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName().matches("%inflate%") or
    mc.getLocation().getStartLine() >= 470 and mc.getLocation().getStartLine() <= 500 or
    mc.getLocation().getStartLine() >= 830 and mc.getLocation().getStartLine() <= 860
  )
select mc, "Zipper." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Checking whether zip extraction in Zipper.java has path traversal protection around the vulnerable spots

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name unzipAll calls newFile protection check
 * @description Check whether unzipAll properly routes through the newFile protection
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/unzipall-newfile-check
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "Zipper" and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() = "unzipAll" and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("newFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith")
  )
select mc, "unzipAll() -> " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Does unzipAll use the newFile() protection or does it bypass it?

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name isFileWithinDirectory uses getAbsoluteFile instead of getCanonicalFile
 * @description Path containment check uses getAbsoluteFile which doesn't resolve .. sequences
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/absolute-not-canonical-path
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

// Find the isFileWithinDirectory method and look for getAbsoluteFile WITHOUT getCanonicalFile
from Method m, MethodCall absoluteCall
where
  m.getName() = "isFileWithinDirectory" and
  m.getDeclaringType().getName() = "LoadPaths" and
  absoluteCall.getMethod().hasName("getAbsoluteFile") and
  absoluteCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  // Confirm no getCanonicalFile is also called in the same method
  not exists(MethodCall canonCall |
    canonCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") and
    canonCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m
  )
select absoluteCall, "isFileWithinDirectory uses getAbsoluteFile() instead of getCanonicalFile() - does not resolve '..' sequences, enabling path traversal"
findings.hypothesis: isFileWithinDirectory uses getAbsoluteFile() instead of getCanonicalFile(), making the startsWith check bypassable with '..' sequences

message.text: isFileWithinDirectory uses getAbsoluteFile() instead of getCanonicalFile() - does not resolve '..' sequences, enabling path traversal

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Callers of isFileWithinDirectory and loadFromLoadPath
 * @description Find what methods call isFileWithinDirectory and the broader call chain
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/load-paths-callers
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

// Find all call sites of isFileWithinDirectory and isOnLoadPath and loadFromLoadPath
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().getName() = "isFileWithinDirectory" or
  mc.getMethod().getName() = "isOnLoadPath" or
  mc.getMethod().getName() = "loadFromLoadPath" or
  mc.getMethod().getName() = "getFile"
select mc, mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() -> " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Understanding the call chain into isFileWithinDirectory to assess attack surface

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name loadFileFromDir uses isFileWithinDirectory with getAbsoluteFile
 * @description The loadFileFromDir method calls isFileWithinDirectory which uses getAbsoluteFile not getCanonicalFile
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/loadfilefromdir-absolute
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

// Look at operations in loadFileFromDir specifically
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "LoadPaths" and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() = "loadFileFromDir" and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getAbsoluteFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getAbsolutePath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("isFileWithinDirectory") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("normalize") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("canonical")
  )
select mc, "loadFileFromDir calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: loadFileFromDir passes paths to isFileWithinDirectory which uses getAbsoluteFile not getCanonicalFile

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name SandboxInterceptor validateFileRead uses isOnLoadPath which calls isFileWithinDirectory with getAbsoluteFile
 * @description Full chain: SandboxInterceptor.validateFileRead -> isOnLoadPath -> isFileWithinDirectory (getAbsoluteFile + startsWith)
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/sandbox-loadpath-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */
import java

// Find methods in SandboxInterceptor that call isOnLoadPath
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "SandboxInterceptor" and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("isOnLoadPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("validateFileRead") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("validateFileWrite") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("loadFromLoadPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getAbsoluteFile") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith")
  )
select mc, "SandboxInterceptor." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() -> " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: SandboxInterceptor file validation uses isOnLoadPath which has the getAbsoluteFile vulnerability
